resolved_rigid_behaviors: target name policy has native editable ignored and priority fields
Magic Shooter spell rows are an editable ordered list with bounded profile-data controls
target selection uses ordered editable HP distance count priority and chase rules
rune and stance actions are editable ordered add remove reorder rules
haste and observed buff families block recast while active
unobservable spell state requires an explicit bounded fallback
legacy scalar stance selection no longer drives runtime
moduleValue no longer contains duplicate domain implementations
diagnostics owns safe API probes and bounded smoke command IO
UI owns style and layout tables
observation adapter owns guarded native reads
Route and CaveBot Runtime own editor bindings and movement state decisions

immediate_slices.title: Ownership and reachability audit

Remove duplicate targeting fallbacks from native shell
Editable ignored and priority target-name policy
Editable ordered Magic Shooter spell-rule list
Ordered target and rune/stance action rules
Data-driven spell state registry and anti-spam decisions
Remove scalar stance fallback and centralize combat bookkeeping
Remove the central duplicate module fallback hub
Move API guards and bounded smoke IO into diagnostics ownership
Close the strict shell budget through module-owned CaveBot, diagnostics, UI and observation contracts
Versioned deterministic rule-set and profile migration
Audit and consolidate vocation profile data before schema growth

phases.title: Helper Simplification And Ownership
phases.objective: Reduce the shell, remove dead paths, and give every UI/runtime concern one owner.
done_gates: no confirmed dead local remains
HUD configuration has one canonical surface
native helper is below 4000 lines and 110 functions
module audit measures ownership and reachability

phases.title: Declarative Action And Condition Engine
phases.objective: Represent actions separately from composable conditions instead of hard-coded branches.
done_gates: typed metrics include HP, mana, monster count, PZ and active conditions
operators include <, <=, =, !=, >= and >
AND/OR groups, cooldown, hysteresis and bounded randomization are supported
profile migration is deterministic and runtime stays default-off

phases.title: Configurable Targeting And Magic Shooter
phases.objective: Replace fixed target and spell behavior with editable ordered rule lists.
done_gates: ignored and priority names are editable
target rules expose distance, HP, count, priority and chase policy
spell/rune/stance rows are addable, removable and reorderable
NPC, player, summon, PZ and exercise-dummy guards remain mandatory

phases.title: Spell State Registry And Anti-Spam
phases.objective: Use observed spell/condition state before dispatching haste, buffs, stances and shields.
done_gates: haste never recasts while proven active
unknown state fails closed or uses an explicit bounded fallback
vocation-specific spell families are data, not runtime branches
state transitions have deterministic replay coverage

phases.title: Canonical Profile Schema And Vocation Packs
phases.objective: Make profiles portable, editable and vocation-aware without duplicating runtime logic.
done_gates: one schema owns defaults and migrations
vocation packs contain data only
unknown/custom server spells remain explicitly editable
old profiles migrate without arming runtime

phases.title: Native Unified Operator UX
phases.objective: Expose the flexible model through one native, uncluttered Helper surface.
done_gates: one settings location per concern
rule editors use proven OTClient widgets and bounded contained navigation
disabled, blocked, stale and active states are distinct
smallest supported viewport remains usable

phases.title: Shared Contracts Without Project Coupling
phases.objective: Share pure condition semantics and parity evidence with Safe while preserving product-local evaluators and independent gates.
done_gates: no Helper runtime file is copied into Safe
shared code is pure, bounded and action-free
Safe evidence cannot satisfy Helper acceptance
contract parity tests cover both adapters

phases.title: Replay, Canary And Rollback Program
phases.objective: Prove the simplified Helper with independent replay, sandbox canaries and rollback evidence.
done_gates: independent runner evidence is current
targeting, spell state and profile migrations pass replay
sandbox gameplay canaries are explicit and bounded
live promotion remains a separate operator approval

phases.title: Minimal Helper Distribution Surface
phases.objective: Ship only loader-reachable Helper code and keep local references, cleanup paths and product packages explicitly separate.
done_gates: unloaded duplicate runtimes are absent from official artifacts
one package inventory owns stage sync enablement and promotion
local-only source is explicit and never enters P14 manifests
stale legacy copies are removed without touching Safe

phases.title: UI Composition And Rule Editor Split
phases.objective: Reduce the UI monolith while preserving the proven OTCv8 programmatic widget and bounded navigation contracts.
done_gates: shared form and rule editor primitives have one owner
tab composition is separate from profile mutation callbacks
default and compact previews retain zero layout issues
no unproven OTUI widget or loader API is introduced

phases.title: Portable Rule Presets And Explainability
phases.objective: Make targeting spell and action rule sets portable and understandable without importing executable code or arming runtime.
done_gates: preset import and export use strict versioned data schemas
unknown fields future versions and executable values fail closed
every rule exposes a deterministic why matched or blocked trace
migration and import never enable runtime

phases.title: Independent Sandbox Acceptance And Rollback
phases.objective: Execute visual in-world canary and rollback acceptance on an isolated current-revision Windows runner.
done_gates: all four P14 acceptance capabilities have signed current evidence
bounded canary abort and rollback paths are exercised
Helper and Safe evidence remain independent
live promotion still requires a separate explicit approval
